title: Iseilema trichopus
taxonomy: Iseilema trichopus is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Andropogoneae. The genus Iseilema is a part of the family Poaceae and is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. Iseilema trichopus is specifically found in tropical Africa and is characterized by its short stature and narrow leaves. This species is also known for its ability to thrive in a variety of environments.

intro: Iseilema trichopus, also known as "Elephant's Tooth," is a plant species belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Iseilema trichopus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
